integration_hint: At integration time, compute the oldest and latest email timestamps from the active Enron slice and keep only financial_snapshots and public_news_events whose dates overlap that email window.

financial_snapshots.label: FY1998 selected financial data
financial_snapshots.summary: Operating revenue was $31.26B, net income was $703M, total assets were $29.35B, shareholders' equity was $7.05B, and diluted EPS was $1.01.

financial_snapshots.label: FY1999 selected financial data
financial_snapshots.summary: Operating revenue was $40.11B, net income was $893M, total assets were $33.38B, shareholders' equity was $9.57B, and diluted EPS was $1.10.

financial_snapshots.label: FY2000 Form 10-K
financial_snapshots.summary: Operating revenue was $100.79B, net income was $979M, total assets were $65.50B, shareholders' equity was $11.47B, and diluted EPS was $1.12.

financial_snapshots.label: Q4 2000 annual earnings release
financial_snapshots.summary: Enron publicly reported recurring annual diluted EPS of $1.47 for 2000 and fourth-quarter diluted EPS of $0.41.

financial_snapshots.label: Q1 2001 earnings release
financial_snapshots.summary: Enron publicly reported first-quarter diluted EPS of $0.47, revenues of $50.1B, and net income of $406M.

financial_snapshots.label: Q2 2001 earnings release
financial_snapshots.summary: Enron publicly reported second-quarter diluted EPS of $0.45 and net income of $404M.

financial_snapshots.label: Q3 2001 loss announcement
financial_snapshots.summary: Enron announced $1.01B of after-tax non-recurring charges, which resulted in a net loss for the quarter.

public_news_events.headline: Vice chairman Cliff Baxter resigned
public_news_events.summary: Enron's archived 2001 press chronology records Cliff Baxter's resignation on May 2, 2001.

public_news_events.headline: Enron announced a third-quarter loss
public_news_events.summary: The company disclosed $1.01B in after-tax non-recurring charges and said the quarter ended in a net loss.

public_news_events.headline: SEC requested information about related-party transactions
public_news_events.summary: Enron said the SEC had asked it to voluntarily provide information about certain related-party transactions.

public_news_events.headline: Board formed a special committee and the SEC opened a formal investigation
public_news_events.summary: Enron said its board appointed a special committee and that the SEC had opened a formal investigation into the matters under review.

public_news_events.headline: Enron said it would restate prior financial statements
public_news_events.summary: Enron said it would restate results from 1997 through 2000 and the first two quarters of 2001 and reflect a $1.2B reduction in shareholders' equity.

public_news_events.headline: Enron announced a proposed sale to Dynegy
public_news_events.summary: Enron reported that Dynegy would acquire the company, subject to shareholder approval and other closing conditions.

public_news_events.headline: Enron entered Chapter 11 proceedings
public_news_events.summary: The SEC proceeding record is used as the official source reference for Enron's Chapter 11 case.
